title: Disynaphia radula
taxonomy: Disynaphia radula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the gentian family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes other families such as Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The order Gentianales is in turn part of the clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of the sunflower and daisy families. The clade Asterids is part of the larger group of flowering plants called angiosperms, which also includes other groups such as monocots and dicots. The classification of Disynaphia radula reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its characteristics as a member of the gentian family.
